attributes.stem: A 27-year-old primagravida woman presents to her general practitioner following two positive urine pregnancy tests. She has a regular 28 days cycle and her last menstrual period was five weeks ago. She is happy with her pregnancy however she is worried regarding the effects that some of her medications might have on the foetus. Her medical history includes major depression, iron deficiency anaemia, and epilepsy managed by levetiracetam. She also takes sertraline, iron tablets, and occasional ibuprofen and paracetamol for tension headaches. Her over the counter medications include vitamin D supplements during winter and daily vitamin B12.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following medications should be discontinued during her pregnancy?
attributes.explanation:
NSAIDs is contraindicated during pregnancy. Early closure of ductus arteriosus and renal toxicity in the foetus is associated with use of non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) during >20 weeks of gestation. This is thought to occur due to the inhibition of prostaglandin, leading to premature closure of ductus arteriosus. It is important to educate pregnant women regarding the effects of NSAID's and recommend safer pain management such as paracetamol especially during third trimester. In this case, the patient does not need to stop the ibuprofen now, but she needs to be counselled to avoid the drug after week 20 of her pregnancy.

Iron tablets and paracetamol are generally safe to take during pregnancy. Repeat blood test and iron studies should be done to determine the severity of her iron deficiency anaemia. Dosing of iron tablets should also be adjusted according to the results of her investigations.

Sertraline is a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI). It is a safe antidepressant to use peripartum. Although the use of sertraline during the second and third trimester is thought to be associated with lower birth weight and mild respiratory distress in newborn, pregnancy is not an absolute contraindication for prescribing sertraline. Sertraline may still be recommended in some pregnant women according to the severity of their symptoms. It is important to weigh the risks and benefit of prescribing sertraline for each patient, and understand the substantial impact of maternal depression on the mother and infant.

Levetiracetam as epilepsy management should be continued during pregnancy. Uncontrolled seizure can be life-threatening for the mother and foetus, therefore most epileptic patients are recommended to continue on at least one epileptic drug. Levetiracetam is an anti-epileptic drug with no known teratogenicity effects, and is usually the recommended anti-epileptic drug to use during pregnancy. It can also be continued when the mother is lactating. It is important to prescribe a higher dose of folate supplements -5mg, for pregnant women with epilepsy diagnosis. Sodium valproate/carbamazepine should also be avoided during pregnancy for the risk of neural tube defects in the newborn.
learningPoints: NSAIDs use during >20 weeks of gestation is associated with early closure of ductus arteriosus and renal toxicity in fetus.
references: Antonucci R, Zaffanello M, Puxeddu E, Porcella A, Cuzzolin L, Dolores Pilloni M, Fanos V. Use of non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs in pregnancy: impact on the fetus and newborn. Current drug metabolism. 2012 May 1;13(4):474-90. Viewed December 2022.
Lander CM, Neurologist SV. Antiepileptic drugs in pregnancy and lactation. Aust Prescr 2008;31:70-2.https://doi.org/10.18773/austprescr.2008.041
Williams AS. Antidepressants in pregnancy and breastfeeding. Aust Prescr 2007;30:125-7. Available at: https://doi.org/10.18773/austprescr.2007.075
The Regents of the University of California. Spina Bifida (Myelomeningocele). UCSF Benioff's Children Hospital. 2022. Viewed December 2022. Available at: https://fetus.ucsf.edu/spina-bifida/
NHS. Spina Bifida. NHS Website. Viewed December 2022. Available at: https://www.nhs.uk/conditions/spina-bifida/causes/
